assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: Response clearly mentions dev clusters on Rapid channel and prod clusters on Stable channel, and includes dev-specific testing in pre-upgrade checklist

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: While it mentions some Autopilot-specific checks like automatic node provisioning, it doesn't explicitly acknowledge key limitations like no SSH access or no direct node management

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: Includes checking for deprecated APIs and validating workload manifests against new API versions in the pre-upgrade checklist

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: While it mentions reviewing resource requests/limits, it doesn't specifically call out that resource requests are mandatory in Autopilot

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: Includes PodDisruptionBudgets, readiness/liveness probes, and handling node disruptions gracefully in the pre-upgrade checklist

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: Includes specific commands like 'kubectl version' and 'gcloud container clusters describe CLUSTER_NAME' for version verification

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: Comprehensive workload health checks including pod status, services, endpoints, and application testing sections

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends verifying dev clusters are running 1.32 and running full test suite against dev clusters first

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: Does not mention the GKE release schedule or the advance notice timing beyond the automatic notification mentioned in the user prompt
Includes extensive monitoring sections covering dashboards, alerts, performance metrics, and logging validation

assertions.expectation: Does NOT proactively recommend node-level maintenance exclusions for Autopilot unless the customer explicitly asks for node upgrade control
assertions.reason: Response correctly does not mention node-level maintenance exclusions, staying focused on Autopilot-appropriate recommendations
